content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Verified all four amendments against source code. Three accepted as real bugs: crash-window duplicate comments (fr-7-a79f285c, currently masked by wrong CLI subcommand), partial mapping loss from coarse boolean fallback guard (fr-7-3afb6840), and incorrect br CLI subcommand (fr-7-d6991eda). One rejected (fr-7-9e0205ad) because its key claims about missing ordering guarantees and missing idempotency guards are factually wrong — list_payloads sorts by created_at and the call-site guard prevents repeated reconstruction.

## Votes

- `fr-7-a79f285c`: Accept
  The crash-window concern is architecturally valid. verify_and_comment_mappings calls comment_bead which invokes exec_tracked_mutation — this executes the br subprocess, posting the comment externally. A crash after comment posting but before persisting mapped_bead_verified=true leaves the journal in an unverified state. On replay, the !mapped_bead_verified filter re-processes the mapping, re-posting the comment (duplicate). The code comments (lines 757-761) describe a persist-before-flush ordering that protects against post-flush crashes, but the pre-persist crash window remains. Currently masked by fr-7-d6991eda (wrong br subcommand means comments fail anyway), but this becomes a real issue once that bug is fixed. Making commenting idempotent or splitting verification from commenting is a sound recommendation.
- `fr-7-9e0205ad`: Reject
  Both key claims are factually incorrect. First, list_payloads in fs.rs explicitly calls records.sort_by_key(|r| r.created_at), guaranteeing chronological order regardless of filesystem listing behavior. The 'wrong aggregate' concern is unfounded. Second, the call site at success_reconciliation.rs:722 checks !has_any_for_bead before calling reconstruct_pe_mappings_from_aggregate — once any mapping exists for the bead, reconstruction is never re-triggered, preventing repeated appends. Unbounded journal duplication cannot occur. The remaining concern about journal cleanliness is acknowledged by the amendment itself as not a correctness bug and is too minor to warrant changes.
- `fr-7-3afb6840`: Accept
  Verified as a real correctness bug. The fallback guard (success_reconciliation.rs:713) uses has_any_for_bead — a boolean check across ALL rounds. If Round 1 successfully records mapping A but Round 2's best-effort recording (engine.rs:2697-2712, with fire-and-forget error handling at ~7647-7662) fails for mappings B and C, the fallback never triggers because mapping A exists. Furthermore, reconstruct_pe_mappings_from_aggregate only examines the latest aggregate (iter().rev().find()), so even if it were somehow called, it would only replay from the newest aggregate. The net effect: accepted planned-elsewhere findings from later rounds can be permanently lost if the journal write fails. The fix should compare journal mappings against the aggregate to identify missing entries rather than using a simple boolean guard.
- `fr-7-d6991eda`: Accept
  Clear, confirmed bug. br_process.rs:227-229 builds BrCommand with Self::new("comment").arg(id).arg(text), producing `br comment <id> <text>`. The actual br CLI requires `br comments add <id> <text>` — confirmed by the authoritative bootstrap script (docs/ralph_milestone_beads_bootstrap_v2.sh:73) which uses `"${BR[@]}" comments add "$id" "$body"`. Every comment_bead call in planned_elsewhere.rs (lines 170-177 and 305-312) fails at runtime with an unrecognized subcommand error. Fix is straightforward: change Self::new("comment") to Self::new("comments").arg("add").
